steps.title: Set up the surface area formula
steps.content: A rectangular prism has three types of faces appearing twice each. With dimensions $l = 4$, $w = 5$, and $h = 6$ inches, we calculate the sum of the three distinct rectangular face areas, then double it.

steps.title: Calculate the three face areas
steps.content:
• Face 1 (4 × 5): $4 \times 5 = 20$ square inches
  • Face 2 (4 × 6): $4 \times 6 = 24$ square inches
  • Face 3 (5 × 6): $5 \times 6 = 30$ square inches
Sum: $20 + 24 + 30 = 74$ square inches.

steps.title: Multiply by 2 and identify the answer
steps.content:
The total surface area accounts for two of each face:
$$SA = 2 \times 74 = 148 \text{ square inches}$$That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
